name: Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour
name_original: Salita epica al Monte Colcurinho – Giro ad anello
region_intro: Road cycling around São Sebastião Da Feira offers diverse landscapes characterized by a mountainous area with medium altitude mountain ranges like Lousã, Açor, and Muradal. The region's distinctive geophysical characteristics are shaped by the predominance of schist, forming unique villages and terrain. Cyclists navigate through lush forests and along riparian galleries, including the S-shaped River Alva. This area provides challenging terrain with epic climbs and varied routes suitable for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around São Sebastião Da Feira The most popular road cycling route is Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour, a 32.2 miles (51.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, testing endurance. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Covão do Ferro Lagoon – Serra da Estrela loop from São Gião, a difficult 50.3 miles (81.0 km) path. This route offers extensive climbs through the Serra da Estrela region, Portugal's largest protected area. Local road cyclists also love the Miradouro do Penedo Cabana loop from Bobadela, a 31.3 miles (50.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around São Sebastião Da Feira is defined by mountainous areas, schist villages, and forested landscapes. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with 13 routes available. The routes in São Sebastião Da Feira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore São Sebastião Da Feira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around São Sebastião Da Feira?
A: There are 13 dedicated road cycling routes around São Sebastião Da Feira, offering a mix of moderate and difficult challenges. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in São Sebastião Da Feira?
A: The region is characterized by a mountainous area with medium-altitude ranges like Lousã, Açor, and Muradal, creating a dramatic 'sea of mountains'. You can expect challenging climbs, lush forests, and routes that often pass through the unique Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages).

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in São Sebastião Da Feira?
A: While the area is known for its challenging terrain and 'epic climbs', there are 6 moderate routes available. These still offer a good workout but are less demanding than the 7 difficult routes, making them suitable for cyclists looking for a less intense experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the mountainous landscape and pass through the unique Aldeias do Xisto. You might encounter the S-shaped River Alva, and some routes lead towards significant attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historical Village of Piódão&lt;/a&gt; or offer views towards the nearby Serra da Estrela Natural Park. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2314117892/epic-climb-to-monte-colcurinho-loop-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour&lt;/a&gt; is a destination in itself for its significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in São Sebastião Da Feira are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2314117892/epic-climb-to-monte-colcurinho-loop-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Epic climb to Monte Colcurinho - Loop tour&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19435410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Ferro Lagoon – Serra da Estrela loop from São Gião&lt;/a&gt; are both challenging loop routes that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in São Sebastião Da Feira?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the 'epic climbs', and the opportunity to explore the unique schist villages and natural beauty of the region.

Q: Is São Sebastião Da Feira a good destination for advanced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. São Sebastião Da Feira is renowned for its challenging terrain, featuring 'epic climbs that recall the mountain stages of the most mythical world races'. With 7 difficult routes, including the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19435410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Ferro Lagoon – Serra da Estrela loop from São Gião&lt;/a&gt;, it's an ideal destination for experienced riders seeking to test their limits against significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, the region offers a mix of moderate and difficult routes. For families with older children or those comfortable with some elevation, the moderate routes could be suitable. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5885127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz d'Égua&lt;/a&gt; river beach, with its facilities, could also be a pleasant stop during a ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in São Sebastião Da Feira?
A: The region's mountainous climate means spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons provide ideal conditions for tackling the climbs and enjoying the scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation tailored for cyclists?
A: São Sebastião Da Feira is part of the 'Cyclin' Portugal' network, which means it's a bike-friendly destination. This includes 'Cyclin' Portugal Centres' with facilities and 'Bikotel' accommodations specifically tailored for cyclists, ensuring you'll find places to rest and refuel.

Q: Can I find parking near the road cycling routes?
A: As a region committed to active tourism and part of the 'Cyclin' Portugal' initiative, you can generally expect to find suitable parking options in and around the villages that serve as starting points for routes. Specific parking details might vary by route, but main villages like São Sebastião Da Feira or Bobadela (starting point for &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32183046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradouro do Penedo Cabana loop from Bobadela&lt;/a&gt;) are good places to look.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages)?
A: Yes, São Sebastião Da Feira is an integral part of the Aldeias do Xisto network, and many road cycling routes are designed to showcase these unique traditional villages. Cycling through these villages offers a glimpse into local heritage and culture, with their distinctive schist architecture.

Monte do Colcurinho is an imposing peak of about 1,242 meters in the Serra do Açor, Portugal. It offers spectacular views that extend from the Serra da Estrela to much of central Portugal. In addition to its natural beauty, it is an important religious site, with a sanctuary dedicated to Nossa Senhora das Necessidades, a destination for annual pilgrimages. It is a place that combines breathtaking nature and deep spirituality.

Piódão belongs to the network of Historic Villages of Portugal.
The town (village) of Piódão, the urban center and the parish headquarters, has been classified as a Property of Public Interest since 1978.[3]
Pedestrian trails of extraordinary beauty.👍🥾🦯
It's a great way to get to Piódão. However, due to the terrible road surface, it's best not to exit here.
